address: 1 Moo 4, Nong Kae, Hua Hin, Prachuap Khiri Khan 77110
entry_fee: 500 THB adults, children under 110 cm free, discounted rate for 110–150 cm
best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings for shorter queues on the popular slides

description.en:
## Hua Hin's Biggest Water Park

Black Mountain Water Park is a 48,000-square-metre water park set against the dramatic backdrop of Hua Hin's limestone hills, located just 15 minutes from the town centre. Since opening in 2011 it has become one of the most popular family attractions in the region, drawing both tourists and Bangkok weekenders.

## Slides and Rides

The park features a 17-metre-high tower with nine different water slides suited to various thrill levels. Highlights include The Bee, a 16-metre-tall slide with a 76-metre-long run, and The Blue Snake, an 86-metre enclosed tunnel slide that twists through complete darkness before splashing out into daylight. For younger children there is a dedicated kids' zone with shallow pools and gentle splash features.

## Wave Pool and Lazy River

The centrepiece is the wave pool, which generates some of the largest artificial waves in Thailand — perfect for body surfing. When you need to unwind, the lazy river offers a gentle float around the park's landscaped tropical gardens. There is also a beach-style infinity pool for those who prefer quiet relaxation.

## Wibit Sports Park

Thrill seekers can upgrade to the inflatable Wibit Sports Park, an obstacle course set on the water featuring climbing walls, trampolines, and balance beams. It is challenging and fun for teenagers and adults alike.

## Practical Details

Lockers and towel hire are available on-site. Outside food and drinks are not permitted, but the park has a restaurant and snack bars with reasonable prices. The park is closed every Wednesday for maintenance.
description.nl: Populair waterpark met glijbanen, golfzwembad en luie rivier tegen een bergachtergrond

tips: Book tickets online through Klook or the park website for discounts of up to 25 percent off walk-in prices
The park closes on Wednesdays — double-check before planning your visit
Bring reef-safe sunscreen and reapply often as most areas are open-air
